etymology.description: Phonetic 爻("yoke") + phonetic 六("six") -> 𦥑 added around to form 𦥯 (concept of learning) -> Semantic 子("child") added -> simplified as 冖("cover") + 爻 -> final form 學
etymology.interpretation: The traditional character 學 (xué, 'learn') combines phonetic elements to suggest sounds related to learning. The addition of 子 (zǐ, 'child') emphasizes the concept of a child gaining knowledge. Over time, the character evolved to include simplified components, but retained its educational theme by metaphorically representing a child learning under a cover, protected and focused.
etymology.simplification: 學 was simplified to 学 by removing 爻 and 子 and merging components to form a more fluid structure, reducing character complexity.

examples.chinese: 我想学钢琴(我想學鋼琴)。
她在学校学习数学(她在學校學習數學)。
我们应该向经验丰富的人学习(我們應該向經驗豐富的人學習)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yīnggāi xiàng jīngyàn fēngfù de rén xuéxí.
examples.english: We should learn from experienced people.
